summary: Iteration 40 — Microsoft 365 SMTP Email + Outbox. Backend pytest 27/27 PASSED. Comprehensive coverage of /api/email/* endpoints: config status, shared-mailbox test (400 when not configured + EmailStr 422), per-user SMTP CRUD with Fernet-encrypted app_password at rest (verified token prefix 'gAAAAA' in MongoDB direct query), idempotent UPSERT on PUT /me/smtp, /me/test with fake creds correctly returns outbox record with status='failed' and friendly error message after SMTP connect fails to smtp.office365.com:587 (~30s), generic /email/send sender='shared'/'user' guards, outbox list pagination + filters + _id exclusion, outbox detail 404, retry resets status to queued, retry rejects already-sent (400), indexes verified (email_outbox.id unique + smtp_user_credentials.user_id unique), RBAC 403 enforced on vendor role across /config, /outbox, /send. Shared mailbox INTENTIONALLY not configured in .env (expected per spec). Fernet key IS configured. No backend code changes required.
action_items: Populate M365_SMTP_SHARED_USERNAME and M365_SMTP_SHARED_PASSWORD in /app/backend/.env once user provides the M365 mailbox + App Password. After that, re-run TestSharedTest with real creds (or accept the 400→200 transition naturally).
(Frontend not in scope this iteration) Build the Outbox UI + Profile→Email Settings panel that hits these endpoints — backend contract is stable and tested.
critical_code_review_comments: m365_email.py: Fernet encrypt/decrypt helpers correctly raise RuntimeError when key not configured. Token format ('gAAAAA…') verified live in DB. Clean separation of SMTPAuthError / SMTPThrottleError / SMTPPermanentError with proper 4xx-vs-5xx classification and AUTH detection (535, 5.7.3, 5.7.30).
send_email() does NOT retry auth or permanent errors — only throttle/unknown — correct exponential backoff with jitter. max_attempts=3, base_delay=2s, caps at 30s.

email_router._send_and_log() handles partial recipient failures (some accepted, some refused) and stores attempts/smtp_response/last_error/sent_at on every code path. _persist_outbox_record consistently updates `status` to 'sent' or 'failed'.
All Mongo queries pass {'_id': 0} projection — verified no _id leakage in list, detail, or me/test responses.

ensure_email_indexes() creates unique indexes on email_outbox.id and smtp_user_credentials.user_id at startup — verified via index_information(). Additional non-unique compound indexes for status/sender_type/queued_by + created_at provide good list-query performance.

UserSmtpUpdate model uses Pydantic EmailStr and 8≤len(app_password)≤200 — good. NOTE: Pydantic's EmailStr rejects reserved TLDs like '.invalid' (had to use a non-reserved domain for fake-creds testing) — not a bug, but worth knowing for future error UX.
PUT /me/smtp uses $setOnInsert for created_at on insert + $set for everything else — proper upsert pattern, no extra round-trips. Idempotency verified (count_documents == 1 after second PUT).

POST /email/send: BackgroundTasks dispatch on the FastAPI background runner — request returns immediately with outbox_id and status='queued'. Good UX. POST /config/test and POST /me/test, by contrast, await _send_and_log inline so the caller sees the final status — appropriate for explicit test buttons.

_queue_outbox attachments_summary correctly base64-decodes only when present (guard against KeyError). attachments_inline is excluded from list AND detail responses — good (large payloads stay out of API responses; still recoverable from DB for actual send).

Minor nit (NOT a blocker): /email/outbox sort('created_at', -1) is fine, but limit() then to_list(limit) is slightly redundant — could just use to_list(None) since limit() is already applied. Cosmetic only.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration40_m365_email.py (new — 27 pytest cases covering all email endpoints, RBAC, Fernet at-rest verification, indexes)
test_credentials: admin@erp.com / Admin@123 (super_admin), TEST_vendor_iter40@erp.com / Vendor@123 (vendor — auto-created via /api/auth/register for the 403 tests)
seed_data_creation: Created vendor user TEST_vendor_iter40@erp.com via super_admin /api/auth/register (left in DB for future re-runs — idempotent). Test data with subject prefix 'Iter40 ' in email_outbox is cleaned in module teardown. Admin's smtp_user_credentials are deleted at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 40 (M365 SMTP Email + Outbox) backend is fully verified. Shared mailbox is INTENTIONALLY unconfigured in /app/backend/.env (user's M365 App Password pending). Tests assert this 'not configured' contract explicitly — DO NOT consider it a bug. Once user populates M365_SMTP_SHARED_USERNAME / M365_SMTP_SHARED_PASSWORD, the /config endpoint's shared_mailbox_configured flips to true and /config/test transitions from 400 to 200/sent. Fernet key M365_FERNET_KEY is set and per-user encryption verified end-to-end (encrypted_app_password in Mongo starts with 'gAAAAA' Fernet token marker, plaintext never present). All 27 pytest cases pass in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration40_m365_email.py — run with: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration40_m365_email.py -v`. Vendor RBAC role TEST_vendor_iter40@erp.com is seeded for repeat runs. No frontend yet — when main agent builds Outbox UI / Profile→Email Settings panel, frontend testing agent should validate against these endpoints.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Two test-script issues were self-fixed during the run: (1) Pydantic EmailStr rejects reserved TLD '.invalid', so the fake-creds test was switched to 'itlis-fake.com' (still resolves to an unreachable / auth-failing SMTP path — the desired test condition). (2) Empty 'to' form field returns 422 (Pydantic) on this FastAPI version instead of 400 — test was relaxed to accept either 4xx code since the contract intent (missing 'to' is rejected) is preserved.
